# WCAG - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/wcag#)

Ionic applications should strive to conform to Web Content Accessibility Guidelines ([WCAG](https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/)
). These guidelines provide practical examples of how to meet those requirements for accessibility but should only be a baseline as many other factors weigh in for accessibility such as performance, UX, usability, etc.

What is WCAG?[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/wcag#what-is-wcag "Direct link to heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Accessibility professionals from the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) published detailed guidelines that define the requirements for web content accessibility called the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG).

The [WCAG](https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag)
 technical documents are developed by the Accessibility Guidelines Working Group (AG WG) (formerly the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines Working Group), which is part of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI).

WCAG is organized around 4 basic principles:

*   **[Perceivable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/#principle1)
    ** - Can users perceive the content? Just because something is perceivable with one sense, such as sight, that doesn’t mean all users can experience it.
*   **[Operable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/#principle2)
    ** - Can users operate an interface or navigate the content? A hover interaction, for instance, cannot be operated by someone who can’t use a mouse or touchpad.
*   **[Understandable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/#principle3)
    ** - Can users understand the content? Is the interface clear, and consistent enough to avoid confusion?
*   **[Robust](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/#principle4)
    ** - Can the content be consumed by a wide variety of user agents (browsers)? Does it work with assistive technology?

Compliance[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/wcag#compliance "Direct link to heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WCAG has levels of conformance with guidelines that fall into Levels A, AA, and AAA. Most countries and companies strive for AA compliance where Level A issues are treated as issues that must be fixed.

*   Level A - Minimal level of Compliance
*   Level AA - Optimal conformance that satisfies Level A and AA.
*   Level AAA - Conformance that satisfies Level A, AA, and AAA but is not recommended as general policy because it is not possible to satisfy all Level AAA success criteria.

*   [What is WCAG?](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/wcag#what-is-wcag)
    
*   [Compliance](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/wcag#compliance)

---

# VoiceOver for iOS - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#)

[VoiceOver](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-practice-voiceover-iph3e2e415f/ios)
 is a gesture based screen reader that is built into iOS devices. You can turn VoiceOver on by asking Siri "Turn On VoiceOver".

Your application is a series of text, buttons and links which will navigated using [VoiceOver Gestures](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/learn-voiceover-gestures-iph3e2e2281/15.0/ios/15.0)
 rather than relying on your eyesight to swipe and tap visual elements. Use the gesture reference below:

Actions[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#actions "Direct link to heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Select an item** - Tap
*   **Activate the selected item** - Double tap
*   **Double-tap the selected item** - Triple-tap
*   **Drag a slider** - Tap the slider to select it, then swipe up or down with one finger; or double-tap and hold the slider until you hear three rising tones, then drag the slider
*   **Start or stop the current action** - Two-finger double-tap. (for example, play or pause music or a video, take a photo in Camera, start or stop a recording, start or stop the stopwatch)
*   **Dismiss an alert or return to the previous screen** - Two-finger scrub (move two fingers back and forth three times quickly, making a “z”)
*   **Edit an item’s label to make it easier to find** - Two-finger double-tap and hold

Navigation[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#navigation "Direct link to heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Select the next item** - Swipe right
*   **Select the previous item** - Swipe left
*   **Move into a group of items** - Two-finger swipe right
*   **Move out of a group of items** - Two-finger swipe left
*   **Select the first item on the screen** - Four-finger tap near the top of the screen
*   **Select the last item on the screen** - Four-finger tap near the bottom of the screen
*   **Speak the entire screen from the top** - Two-finger swipe up
*   **Speak the entire screen from the selected item** - Two-finger swipe down
*   **Pause or continue speaking** - Two-finger tap
*   **Speak additional information** - Three-finger tap (Such as the position within a list)

Scrolling[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#scrolling "Direct link to heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Scroll up one page** - Three-finger swipe down
*   **Scroll down one page** - Three-finger swipe up
*   **Scroll left one page** - Three-finger swipe right
*   **Scroll right one page** - Three-finger swipe left

VoiceOver Rotor[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#voiceover-rotor "Direct link to heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The VoiceOver Rotor is a key component for iOS accessibility that is often overlooked. It allows user to change VoiceOver to focus on specific content types.

To use the rotor on iOS rotate 2 fingers as if you are turning a dial.

*   [Actions](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#actions)
    
*   [Navigation](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#navigation)
    
*   [Scrolling](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#scrolling)
    
*   [VoiceOver Rotor](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover#voiceover-rotor)

---

# Keyboard - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#)

Many users with motor disabilities rely on the keyboard as well as many blind users. For many enterprise applications efficiency may be the reason to use the keyboard. As an Ionic application can target the web its important to test for keyboard accessibility.

The first step in testing keyboard accessibility is to pressing the Tab key to see what interactive elements you can and cannot reach.

Testing can be done in any web browser or mobile device with a bluetooth keyboard.

You may need to enable tabbing to ensure all interactive controls will focus:

*   **For Mac** - Visit `System Preferences` > `Keyboard` > `Shortcuts` > `Keyboard`.
*   **For Safari** - Visit `Safari` > `Preferences` > `Advanced` and check the box `Press Tab to highlight each item on a webpage`

Hidden Controls[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#hidden-controls "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you paste the following line in your dev tools console you can see whenever the active control changes and it will reveal controls that are focused but hidden:

    document.body.addEventListener('focusin', () => console.log(document.activeElement));

Copy

Tips[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#tips "Direct link to heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Setting `opacity` to `none` or `height` / `width` to `0` is often used to hide elements but they are still focusable with the keyboard. Use `display: none` to truly hide elements.
*   A typical cause for hidden controls is the css `outline` set to `none` or `0`. Consider custom styling outlines instead.
*   The `div` element is not focusable. If a `div` has interactivity then it needs to be fixed (eg by changing to `button` and changing the style).
*   Test using arrow keys as well as the tab key. Not every element accessible via tab can be accessed via arrow keys (and should be).

You can override default styling for a `button` when you replace a `div`. For example:

    background-color: transparent;border: none;border-radius: 0;

Copy

Testing Escape[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#testing-escape "Direct link to heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Pressing the `Esc` key on the keyboard will close Ionic components like an `alertController`, `ion-picker`, `ion-menu` etc. Make sure to test by pressing `Esc` on elements you have written and handle by using the `keyup` event and also be sure to `focus()` where the user should navigate from next.

Ionic Component Styling[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#ionic-component-styling "Direct link to heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can add css styling to Ionic components so that during keyboard navigation the focused element is easily visible. For example, this css will show a red border around any element:

    ion-input.has-focus,button:focus-visible,ion-button.ion-focused,ion-segment-button.ion-focused,ion-radio.ion-focused,ion-select.ion-focused,ion-fab.ion-focused,ion-searchbar.ion-focused,ion-checkbox.ion-focused,ion-toggle.ion-focused,ion-range.ion-focused,ion-datetime.ion-focused,ion-accordion.ion-focused,ion-textarea.ion-focused {  outline: 2px solid red;}

Copy

For the above css to work there are some components you will need to add `class="ion-focusable"` to. This includes: `ion-fab`, `ion-searchbar`, `ion-checkbox`, `ion-toggle`, `ion-range`, `ion-datetime`, `ion-accordion`, and `ion-textarea`. For example:

    <ion-textarea class="ion-focusable"></ion-textarea>

Copy

##### tip

One important difference between using a psuedoclass like `ion-focused` and [`focus`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/:focus)
 is that `focus` will style the element whether the user focused it through keyboard or by clicking on it. Using `ion-focused` will mean that the element is only styled when it is focused via the keyboard.

Non-interactive Components[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#non-interactive-components "Direct link to heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Some components like `ion-badge` are not interactive but you may want to make them interactive by allowing a user to click on them. Example:

    <ion-badge (click)="dostuff()">33</ion-badge>

Copy

But, this component cannot be reached through keyboard interaction. So it is better to refactor this to:

    <button (click)="dostuff()"><ion-badge>33</ion-badge></button>

Copy

You will need to style using `focus-visible` for the focus area to be visible:

    button:focus-visible {  outline: 2px solid red;}

Copy

Focusing Non-interactive Elements[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#focusing-non-interactive-elements "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you have a non-interactive element like a `div` or `span` you can focus it by using the `focus()` method. However, because these elements are not interactive a screen reader will not read the element and the focus will not change.

You can set `tabindex="0"` on the element to allow it to focus.

    <span tabindex="0" #container class="error"> {{ errorMessage }} </span>

Copy

You could also set `role="button"` however, in general it is not a good idea to make non-interactive elements focusable as it interrupts the natural flow of interactive elements, so use this technique sparingly.

*   [Hidden Controls](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#hidden-controls)
    
*   [Tips](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#tips)
    
*   [Testing Escape](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#testing-escape)
    
*   [Ionic Component Styling](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#ionic-component-styling)
    
*   [Non-interactive Components](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#non-interactive-components)
    
*   [Focusing Non-interactive Elements](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/keyboard#focusing-non-interactive-elements)

---

# Use Version 7 - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#)

Ionic Framework v7 introduces a simplification that will mean you can replace use of `ion-label` with a `label` property (and in some cases removing `ion-item` and `ion-list` as well).

This change also improves how a screen reader will announce the component by treating the label as as part of the form control.

Here are some examples of replacing Ionic v6 style markup for the newer v7 style:

ion-input[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-input "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>    <ion-label position="floating">Your Name</ion-label>    <ion-input name="name" [(ngModel)]="name"></ion-input></ion-item>

Copy

We replace `ion-label` for the `label` property and use `labelPlacement` instead of `position`.

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>    <ion-input label="Your Name" labelPlacement="floating" name="name" [(ngModel)]="name"></ion-input></ion-item>

Copy

ion-checkbox[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-checkbox "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before-1 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>   <ion-checkbox [(ngModel)]="terms" slot="start"></ion-checkbox>   <ion-label>I agree to the terms and conditions</ion-label></ion-item>

Copy

Here `labelPlacement` of `end` ensures the label comes after the checkbox and `justify` to set to `start` makes the label left justified.

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after-1 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>    <ion-checkbox labelPlacement="end" justify="start" [(ngModel)]="terms">I agree to the terms and conditions</ion-checkbox></ion-item>

Copy

ion-textarea[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-textarea "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before-2 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>   <ion-label>More Info</ion-label>   <ion-textarea placeholder="Enter more information here..."></ion-textarea>    </ion-item>

Copy

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after-2 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>   <ion-textarea label="More Info" placeholder="Enter more information here..."></ion-textarea>    </ion-item>

Copy

ion-radio[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-radio "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before-3 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-radio-group>    <ion-list-header>        <ion-label>Toppings</ion-label>    </ion-list-header>        <ion-item>        <ion-label>Sprinkles</ion-label>        <ion-radio value="sprinkles"></ion-radio>    </ion-item>    <ion-item>        <ion-label>Marshmallows</ion-label>        <ion-radio value="marshmallows"></ion-radio>    </ion-item>    <ion-item>        <ion-label>Cherries</ion-label>        <ion-radio value="cherries"></ion-radio>    </ion-item></ion-radio-group>

Copy

We replace each `ion-label` and place our label in the `ion-radio`.

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after-3 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-radio-group>   <ion-list-header>      <ion-label>Toppings</ion-label>   </ion-list-header>   <ion-list>      <ion-item>         <ion-radio value="sprinkles">Sprinkles</ion-radio>      </ion-item>      <ion-item>         <ion-radio value="marshmallows">Marshmallows</ion-radio>      </ion-item>      <ion-item>         <ion-radio value="cherries">Cherries</ion-radio>      </ion-item>   </ion-list></ion-radio-group>

Copy

ion-toggle[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-toggle "Direct link to heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before-4 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-list>  <ion-item>    <ion-label>Checked Toggle</ion-label>    <ion-toggle slot="end" [checked]="true"></ion-toggle>  </ion-item></ion-list>

Copy

We replace `ion-list` and `ion-item` with a single `ion-toggle`:

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after-4 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-toggle [checked]="true">Checked Toggle</ion-toggle>

Copy

ion-select[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-select "Direct link to heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Before[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#before-5 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>   <ion-label>Fruit</ion-label>   <ion-select aria-label="fruit" placeholder="Select fruit">      <ion-select-option value="apples">Apples</ion-select-option>      <ion-select-option value="oranges">Oranges</ion-select-option>      <ion-select-option value="bananas">Bananas</ion-select-option>   </ion-select></ion-item>

Copy

We move the contents of `ion-label` into a `label` property on `ion-select`:

#### After[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#after-5 "Direct link to heading")

    <ion-item>      <ion-select label="Fruit" aria-label="fruit" placeholder="Select fruit">      <ion-select-option value="apples">Apples</ion-select-option>      <ion-select-option value="oranges">Oranges</ion-select-option>      <ion-select-option value="bananas">Bananas</ion-select-option>   </ion-select></ion-item>

Copy

*   [ion-input](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-input)
    
*   [ion-checkbox](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-checkbox)
    
*   [ion-textarea](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-textarea)
    
*   [ion-radio](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-radio)
    
*   [ion-toggle](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-toggle)
    
*   [ion-select](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/ionic-v7#ion-select)

---

# Ionic DevApp - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/devapp#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The Ionic DevApp was a free app used to run your Ionic app directly on your iOS or Android device.

On January 1st, 2020, DevApp was retired as we move toward embracing native tooling and building apps with [Capacitor](http://capacitor.ionicframework.com/)
, Ionic's official native app runtime.

One of the key mantras of Capacitor is that developers should embrace native tools like Android Studio and Xcode when building their app. While using native tooling may initially seem daunting, we think this is the right approach, because it makes it easy to follow existing Native iOS/Android guides, get help on Stack Overflow, and have full control over your project. The reality is that DevApp only got in the way of building an app, delaying developers from seeing their app run on a simulator or phone.

In practice, native tooling is quite easy to use: see our [iOS](https://ionicframework.com/docs/developing/ios)
 and [Android](https://ionicframework.com/docs/developing/android)
 documentation for details on how to build native apps using Cordova or Capacitor.

Native Cordova Plugin Support[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/devapp#native-cordova-plugin-support "Direct link to Native Cordova Plugin Support")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For reference, Ionic DevApp previously supported the following plugins:

    card.io.cordova.mobilesdk 2.1.0 "CardIO"com-intel-security-cordova-plugin 2.0.3 "APP Security API"com.darktalker.cordova.screenshot 0.1.5 "Screenshot"com.paypal.cordova.mobilesdk 3.5.0 # Screen Readers for Web - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/web#)

Ionic applications usually target mobile so testing your app via [VoiceOver](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/voiceover)
 and [TalkBack](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/talkback)
 will cover most scenarios. However, you can target the web in general as well and this means that your users may be on Windows, Mac or Linux desktops and laptops.

Use analytics from your app to see what devices your users are using and test appropriately. You can also take a look a the [WebAIM Screen Reader Survey](https://webaim.org/projects/screenreadersurvey9/)
 to get an idea of readers used.

Tools[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/web#tools "Direct link to heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here are some popular choices:

*   [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org/download/)
    , [Jaws](https://www.freedomscientific.com/products/software/jaws/)
     and [Fusion](https://www.freedomscientific.com/products/software/fusion/)
     for Windows
*   [VoiceOver](https://support.apple.com/guide/voiceover/welcome/mac)
     for Mac

*   [Tools](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/web#tools)

---

# Workarounds - Accessibility Guide

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#)

There are some techniques that are do not follow ARIA/WCAG or are workarounds to improve accessibility.

These are not recommended practices but they "get the job done".

Overriding Visible Text[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#overriding-visible-text "Direct link to heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An `aria-label` only works for interactive elements. So, HTML like this will unfortunately be announced as "3" by the screen reader:

    <ion-label aria-label="3 bedrooms">3</ion-label>

Copy

You can have the screen reader announce something different from the visible text using the following HTML:

    <div role="text" aria-label="3 bedrooms">  <ion-label aria-hidden="true">3</ion-label></div>

Copy

##### warning

`role="text"` is not a recognised aria attribute, however it is implemented by Voice Over in iOS and TalkBack in Android.

A screen reader should read the visible text, so in the above example you should make the visible text `3 bedrooms` instead of `3`. You would only use this technique if your UI design takes precedence over accessibility design.

Reading Formatted Text[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#reading-formatted-text "Direct link to heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Voice Over will break apart text that contains formatting. For example:

    <ion-text>I like <strong>big</strong> brown <em>bears</em>.</ion-text>

Copy

The above text will be read as: "I like. Big. Brown. Bears.".

To ensure that Voice Over reads this sentence as "I like big brown bears." we can set `role="text"`:

    <ion-text role="text">I like <strong>big</strong> brown <em>bears</em>.</ion-text>

Copy

##### warning

`role="text"` is not a recognised aria attribute, however it is implemented by Voice Over in iOS and TalkBack in Android.

Page Titles[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#page-titles "Direct link to heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TalkBack on Android will attempt to announce the page's title. You can use this to describe the page the user is on.

In an Angular application you can set the page title in your routing. For example in `app-routes.ts` or `app-routing-module.ts`:

    {    path: 'intro',    title: 'Introduction',    loadComponent:...  },

Copy

You can also set the title programatically in Angular:

    import {Title} from "@angular/platform-browser";....constructor(private title:Title) {  this.title.setTitle("Introduction Part 1 of 3");}

Copy

A vanilla javascript alternative may be:

    document.title = 'Introduction';

Copy

##### note

TalkBack will read the document title followed by "WebView". There is no known option to change this text.

First Read Element on Routing[​](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#first-read-element-on-routing "Direct link to heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The screen reader will select the first element it wants to read when you route between pages.

On Android this will be the page itself and after swiping right TalkBack will select a focusable element in the top left of the screen.

On iOS the first element may appear to be random but it chooses an element close to where you have pressed before. This behavior is an undesirable quirk of VoiceOver.

Ionic Framework version 8 introduces [`focusManagerPriority`](https://ionicframework.com/docs/developing/managing-focus#assistive-technology-focus-management)
 as a solution to ensure that the desired element is focused when the route changes.

To use this feature you can modify your Ionic config to set `focusManagerPriority`. For example in `main.ts` in an Angular application:

    provideIonicAngular({ focusManagerPriority: ['heading', 'content'] }),

Copy

Now, whenever the route changes the focus will go the first heading element.

##### note

`focusManagerPriority` is currently marked as [Experimental](https://ionicframework.com/docs/developing/config#ionicconfig)
. Be sure to report any issues you may encounter.

*   [Overriding Visible Text](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#overriding-visible-text)
    
*   [Reading Formatted Text](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#reading-formatted-text)
    
*   [Page Titles](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#page-titles)
    
*   [First Read Element on Routing](https://ionic.io/docs/accessibility/workarounds#first-read-element-on-routing)

---

# Install Errors - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/install-errors#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

> error: Appflow CLI is already installed at '/usr/local/bin/appflow'

This error can occur if you have several steps in your CI/CD that install the Appflow CLI. You should ensure that you only install the CLI one time.

* * *

> error: your platform and architecture ... is unsupported.

The Appflow CLI supports the following architectures:

*   macOS (Intel)
*   macOS (Apple M1)
*   Linux (32-bit)
*   Linux (64-bit)
*   Linux (64-bit ARM)

* * *

> error: Appflow CLI version ... was not found.

This error can occur if you specify a version that is no longer available, or the version you specify is improperly formatted. The supported format is "X.X.X" to request an absolute version.

---

# CLI Errors - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Token Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#token-errors "Direct link to Token Errors")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: no personal access token found. To learn how to obtain and configure a token, visit [https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication)

This error can occur when you fail to provide a `TOKEN` line in your config file, and also do not pass a  
`--token` on the command line.

Argument Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#argument-errors "Direct link to Argument Errors")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: invalid argument\[s\] ... for <command>, expected <#> arg(s) \[from ...\]

This error can occur in any circumstance where you provide either the wrong number of arguments to a command, or a value that is not one of the allowed values.

* * *

> Error: open <filepath>: no such file or directory

This error occurs when either an input file is not found at a given filepath or the specified parent directory does not exist for a given output path.

Config Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#config-errors "Direct link to Config Errors")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: Failed to load config <filepath>  
> While parsing config: yaml: line <#>: could not find expected ':'  
> Please visit [https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication)
>  to see examples of a valid config file

This error occurs when you have a line in your config file that is not well-formatted.

* * *

> Error: Failed to load config <filepath>  
> While parsing config: yaml: unmarshal errors: line <#>: cannot unmarshal !!str <config> into map\[string\]interface  
> Please visit [https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication)
>  to see examples of a valid config file

This error occurs when you only have a single line in your config file and it is not well-formatted.

Environment Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#environment-errors "Direct link to Environment Errors")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: Environment variables must have a name

This error occurs when an environment file contains lines like `=VALUE` with no variable name.

* * *

> Error: Environment variable names cannot contain whitespace

This error occurs when an environment file contains lines like `MY VAR NAME=VALUE`.

* * *

> Error: Environment file does not exist: <filepath>

This error can occur when the environment file is not found at the provided path. You should make sure the command is running in the expected directory.

* * *

> Error: Permission denied trying to open environment file: <filepath>

This error can occur when the user executing the Appflow CLI command does not have read permissions for the specified environment file. You should run `ls -al` on the file and `whoami` to make sure the command is running as the right user, and that user has read permission.

* * *

> Error: Max environment variable length exceeded reading environment file

While unlikely to occur, adding lines longer than 64kb to your environment file will make it fail to load.

Contents
--------

*   [Argument Errors](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#argument-errors)
    
*   [Config Errors](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/cli-errors#config-errors)

# API Errors - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

CLI Access Error[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#cli-access-error "Direct link to CLI Access Error")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: Usage of Web Builds, Live Updates, Native Builds, and Deploy to App Store from the Appflow CLI is only available on the Standard and Enterprise Plans. Reach out to Ionic here: [https://ionic.io/pricing](https://ionic.io/pricing)

In order to use the Appflow CLI to integrate with your own CI/CD and other automations, you must use a token associated with a Standard or Enterprise plan organization.

Build Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#build-errors "Direct link to Build Errors")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: Build failed, see above

This error can occur whenever a build fails to complete. Usually, there should be logging output above that can be used to understand why the build failed.

Resource Not Found Errors[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#resource-not-found-errors "Direct link to Resource Not Found Errors")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> Error: Not Authorized: Missing Rule: apps:app\_id:ReadApp

This error can occur if you provide the incorrect `app-id` to a command, or an `app-id` that the token you are using does not have access to.

* * *

> Error: Commit <commit> for app <app-id> not found

This error can occur if you pass a commit SHA that is too short and therefore not unique, or an unknown commit SHA.

Contents
--------

*   [CLI Access Error](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#cli-access-error)
    
*   [Build Errors](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#build-errors)
    
*   [Resource Not Found Errors](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/errors/api-errors#resource-not-found-errors)

1.0.3[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#103 "Direct link to 1.0.3")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Add long description Live Update upload artifact command [docs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_live-update_upload-artifact)
    

1.0.2[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#102 "Direct link to 1.0.2")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bug Fixes[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#bug-fixes-2 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Fix for using the new Appflow CLI install script to install < 1.0 versions of the CLI

1.0.1[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#101 "Direct link to 1.0.1")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bug Fixes[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#bug-fixes-3 "Direct link to Bug Fixes")

*   Avoid resetting the sudo session during the install script so that the user is only required to enter their password once

1.0.0[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#100 "Direct link to 1.0.0")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Features[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#features-3 "Direct link to Features")

*   Ionic Cloud CLI (`ionic-cloud`) has been renamed to the Appflow CLI (`appflow`)
*   Add native config commands [docs](http://localhost:3000/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_native-config)
    
*   The get destination command should return the artifact type [docs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_destination_get)
    
*   Add `--rename` flag to iOS destination update command [docs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_destination_update-ios)
    
*   Add `--rename` flag to Android destination update command [docs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_destination_update-android)
    
*   Include artifacts in get build command [docs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_build_get)
    

### BREAKING CHANGES[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/changelog#breaking-changes "Direct link to BREAKING CHANGES")

*   Remove deprecated `delete build` command
*   Remove deprecated `destination create channel` command
*   Remove deprecated `get build` command

# Camera API Plugin: Capacitor and Cordova for Ionic Apps

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#)

##### EOL Notice

Camera will reach its end-of-life on July 1, 2024, and will no longer receive updates or support. Please see [Support Policy](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/support-policy)
 for additional information.

The Camera plugin offers the ability to take a photo, capture video and choose images from the system's image library.

Installation[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#installation "Direct link to heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you have not already setup Ionic Enterprise in your app, [follow the one-time setup steps](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/setup)
.

Next, install the plugin:

*   Capacitor
*   Cordova

    npm install @ionic-enterprise/cameranpx cap sync

Copy

    ionic cordova plugin add @ionic-enterprise/camera 

Copy

#### Preferences (iOS)[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#preferences-ios "Direct link to heading")

*   **CameraUsesGeolocation** (boolean, defaults to false). For capturing JPEGs, set to true to get geolocation data in the EXIF header. This will trigger a request for geolocation permissions if set to true.

##### Cordova Usage[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#cordova-usage "Direct link to heading")

Add the preference in the `config.xml`

`<preference name="CameraUsesGeolocation" value="false" />`

##### Capacitor Usage[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#capacitor-usage "Direct link to heading")

Add the preference in the `capacitor.config.json`

    "cordova": {  "preferences": {    "CameraUsesGeolocation": "false"  }}

Copy

#### Using with AndroidX Projects (Android)[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#using-with-androidx-projects-android "Direct link to heading")

This plugin relies on the legacy Android Support libraries and will not work in projects using the newer AndroidX libraries without using the [jetifier](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jetifier)
 tool to patch them.

    npm install jetifiernpx jetifier

Copy

To run it automatically when dependencies are installed, add `"postinstall": "jetifier"` in the `package.json`.

Classes[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#classes-1 "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Camera[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#camera-1 "Direct link to heading")

**Camera**:

_**name**_: Camera

_**description**_: Take a photo or capture video.

\[Warning\] Since IOS 10 the camera requires permissions to be placed in your config.xml add

    <config-file parent="NSCameraUsageDescription" platform="ios" target="*-Info.plist"> <string>You can take photos</string></config-file>

Copy

inside of the <platform name='ios> section

_**usage**_:

    import { Camera, CameraOptions } from '@ionic-enterprise/camera/ngx';constructor(private camera: Camera) { }...const options: CameraOptions = { quality: 100, destinationType: this.camera.DestinationType.FILE_URI, encodingType: this.camera.EncodingType.JPEG, mediaType: this.camera.MediaType.PICTURE}this.camera.getPicture(options).then((imageData) => {// imageData is either a base64 encoded string or a file URI// If it's base64 (DATA_URL):let base64Image = 'data:image/jpeg;base64,' + imageData;}, (err) => {// Handle error});

Copy

_**interfaces**_: CameraOptions CameraPopoverOptions

### DestinationType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#destinationtype-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● DestinationType**: _`object`_

Constant for possible destination types

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration "Direct link to heading")

DATA\_URL: `number`

Return base64 encoded string. DATA\_URL can be very memory intensive and cause app crashes or out of memory errors. Use FILE\_URI or NATIVE\_URI if possible

FILE\_URI: `number`

Return file uri (content://media/external/images/media/2 for Android)

NATIVE\_URI: `number`

Return native uri (eg. asset-library://... for iOS)

* * *

### Direction[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#direction-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● Direction**: _`object`_

Convenience constant

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration-1 "Direct link to heading")

BACK: `number`

Use the back-facing camera

FRONT: `number`

Use the front-facing camera

* * *

### EncodingType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#encodingtype-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● EncodingType**: _`object`_

Convenience constant

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration-2 "Direct link to heading")

JPEG: `number`

Return JPEG encoded image

PNG: `number`

Return PNG encoded image

* * *

### MediaType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#mediatype-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● MediaType**: _`object`_

Convenience constant

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration-3 "Direct link to heading")

ALLMEDIA: `number`

Allow selection from all media types

PICTURE: `number`

Allow selection of still pictures only. DEFAULT. Will return format specified via DestinationType

VIDEO: `number`

Allow selection of video only, ONLY RETURNS URL

* * *

### PictureSourceType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#picturesourcetype-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● PictureSourceType**: _`object`_

Convenience constant

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration-4 "Direct link to heading")

CAMERA: `number`

Take picture from camera

PHOTOLIBRARY: `number`

Choose image from picture library (same as PHOTOLIBRARY for Android)

SAVEDPHOTOALBUM: `number`

Choose image from picture library (same as SAVEDPHOTOALBUM for Android)

* * *

### PopoverArrowDirection[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#popoverarrowdirection-1 "Direct link to heading")

**● PopoverArrowDirection**: _`object`_

Convenience constant

#### Type declaration[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#type-declaration-5 "Direct link to heading")

ARROW\_ANY: `number`

ARROW\_DOWN: `number`

ARROW\_LEFT: `number`

ARROW\_RIGHT: `number`

ARROW\_UP: `number`

* * *

### cleanup[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#cleanup "Direct link to heading")

▸ **cleanup**(): `Promise`<`any`\>

Remove intermediate image files that are kept in temporary storage after calling camera.getPicture. Applies only when the value of Camera.sourceType equals Camera.PictureSourceType.CAMERA and the Camera.destinationType equals Camera.DestinationType.FILE\_URI.

**Returns:** `Promise`<`any`\>

* * *

### getPicture[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#getpicture "Direct link to heading")

▸ **getPicture**(options?: _[CameraOptions](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#cameraoptions)
_): `Promise`<`any`\>

Take a picture or video, or load one from the library.

**Parameters:**

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `Optional` options | [CameraOptions](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#cameraoptions) |

**Returns:** `Promise`<`any`\> Returns a Promise that resolves with Base64 encoding of the image data, or the image file URI, depending on cameraOptions, otherwise rejects with an error.

* * *

* * *

Interfaces[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#interfaces-1 "Direct link to heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### CameraOptions[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#cameraoptions "Direct link to heading")

**CameraOptions**:

### `<Optional>` allowEdit[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-allowedit "Direct link to heading")

**● allowEdit**: _`boolean`_

Allow simple editing of image before selection.

* * *

### `<Optional>` cameraDirection[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-cameradirection "Direct link to heading")

**● cameraDirection**: _`number`_

Choose the camera to use (front- or back-facing). Defined in Camera.Direction. Default is BACK. BACK: 0 FRONT: 1

* * *

### `<Optional>` correctOrientation[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-correctorientation "Direct link to heading")

**● correctOrientation**: _`boolean`_

Rotate the image to correct for the orientation of the device during capture.

* * *

### `<Optional>` destinationType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-destinationtype "Direct link to heading")

**● destinationType**: _`number`_

Choose the format of the return value. Defined in Camera.DestinationType. Default is FILE\_URI. DATA\_URL : 0, Return image as base64-encoded string (DATA\_URL can be very memory intensive and cause app crashes or out of memory errors. Use FILE\_URI or NATIVE\_URI if possible), FILE\_URI : 1, Return image file URI, NATIVE\_URI : 2 Return image native URI (e.g., assets-library:// on iOS or content:// on Android)

* * *

### `<Optional>` encodingType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-encodingtype "Direct link to heading")

**● encodingType**: _`number`_

Choose the returned image file's encoding. Defined in Camera.EncodingType. Default is JPEG JPEG : 0 Return JPEG encoded image PNG : 1 Return PNG encoded image

* * *

### `<Optional>` mediaType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-mediatype "Direct link to heading")

**● mediaType**: _`number`_

Set the type of media to select from. Only works when PictureSourceType is PHOTOLIBRARY or SAVEDPHOTOALBUM. Defined in Camera.MediaType PICTURE: 0 allow selection of still pictures only. DEFAULT. Will return format specified via DestinationType VIDEO: 1 allow selection of video only, WILL ALWAYS RETURN FILE\_URI ALLMEDIA : 2 allow selection from all media types

* * *

### `<Optional>` popoverOptions[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-popoveroptions "Direct link to heading")

**● popoverOptions**: _[CameraPopoverOptions](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#camerapopoveroptions)
_

iOS-only options that specify popover location in iPad. Defined in CameraPopoverOptions.

* * *

### `<Optional>` quality[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-quality "Direct link to heading")

**● quality**: _`number`_

Picture quality in range 0-100. Default is 50

* * *

### `<Optional>` saveToPhotoAlbum[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-savetophotoalbum "Direct link to heading")

**● saveToPhotoAlbum**: _`boolean`_

Save the image to the photo album on the device after capture.

* * *

### `<Optional>` sourceType[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-sourcetype "Direct link to heading")

**● sourceType**: _`number`_

Set the source of the picture. Defined in Camera.PictureSourceType. Default is CAMERA. PHOTOLIBRARY : 0, CAMERA : 1, SAVEDPHOTOALBUM : 2

* * *

### `<Optional>` targetHeight[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-targetheight "Direct link to heading")

**● targetHeight**: _`number`_

Height in pixels to scale image. Must be used with targetWidth. Aspect ratio remains constant.

* * *

### `<Optional>` targetWidth[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#optional-targetwidth "Direct link to heading")

**● targetWidth**: _`number`_

Width in pixels to scale image. Must be used with targetHeight. Aspect ratio remains constant.

* * *

* * *

### CameraPopoverOptions[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#camerapopoveroptions "Direct link to heading")

**CameraPopoverOptions**:

iOS-only parameters that specify the anchor element location and arrow direction of the popover when selecting images from an iPad's library or album.

### arrowDir[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#arrowdir "Direct link to heading")

**● arrowDir**: _`number`_

Direction the arrow on the popover should point. Defined in Camera.PopoverArrowDirection Matches iOS UIPopoverArrowDirection constants. ARROW\_UP : 1, ARROW\_DOWN : 2, ARROW\_LEFT : 4, ARROW\_RIGHT : 8, ARROW\_ANY : 15

* * *

### height[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#height "Direct link to heading")

**● height**: _`number`_

* * *

### width[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#width "Direct link to heading")

**● width**: _`number`_

* * *

### x[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#x "Direct link to heading")

**● x**: _`number`_

* * *

### y[​](https://ionic.io/docs/supported-plugins/camera#y "Direct link to heading")

**● y**: _`number`_

* * *

* * *

     Added build badges for **iOS 9.3** and **iOS 10.0**
*   [CB-12312](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CB-12312)
     \[Appium\]\[Android\] A few changes to the tests: - updated comments on how to run the tests. extra comments around functionality at certain points in the automation. - stub of a resolution checker on test startup - still need to figure out acceptable values. - moved session shutdown to an afterAll clause. - changed resolution determiner from using webview-based values to using the native windows dimensions - this helps as the webview values may be scaled down intentionally by manufacturers (via changing devicePixelRatio). furthermore, since the screen dimension automation is used purely for native UI automation, better to use the dimensions reported by the native context rather than the web context. - when finding elements by XPath, use multiple calls to avoid a Windows emulator + Android bug. Made this pattern consistent in the entire test.
*   [CB-12236](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CB-12236)
     - Fixed RELEASENOTES for cordova-plugin-camera
*   [CB-12230](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CB-12230)
     Removed Windows 8.1 build badges

# Migrate from Ionic CLI - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Install[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#install "Direct link to Install")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To install Appflow CLI, run the following command in your terminal:

    curl -fsSL https://ionic.io/get-appflow-cli | bash

After installing, you'll need a [Personal Access Token](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/personal-access-tokens)
 before you get started.

Usage[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#usage "Direct link to Usage")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Web Build & Deploy[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#web-build--deploy "Direct link to Web Build & Deploy")

    ionic deploy build &lt;OPTIONS> ...

is now

    appflow build web &lt;OPTIONS> ...  # Build web artifactappflow deploy web &lt;OPTIONS> ... # Deploy live update

#### Notes:[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#notes "Direct link to Notes:")

*   `--app-id` is currently required.
*   `--channel` is renamed to `--destination` and is only available on `deploy web`.
*   `--skip-download` is no longer an option, instead you must specify which artifacts you wish to download. e.g. `--zip`

### Native Build[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#native-build "Direct link to Native Build")

    ionic package build [ios|android] &lt;OPTIONS> ...

is now

    appflow build [ios|android] &lt;OPTIONS> ...

#### Notes:[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#notes-1 "Direct link to Notes:")

*   `--app-id` is currently required.
*   `--signing-certificate` has been renamed to `--signing-cert`.
*   `--skip-download` is no longer an option, instead you must specify which artifacts you wish to download. e.g. `--aab` or `--ipa`

### Native Deploy[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#native-deploy "Direct link to Native Deploy")

    ionic package deploy [ios|android] &lt;OPTIONS> ...

is now

    appflow deploy [ios|android] &lt;OPTIONS> ...

#### Notes:[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#notes-2 "Direct link to Notes:")

*   `--app-id` is currently required.

Contents
--------

*   [Install](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#install)
    
*   [Usage](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#usage)
    *   [Web Build & Deploy](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#web-build--deploy)
        
    *   [Native Build](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#native-build)
        
    *   [Native Deploy](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/migrate-from-ionic-cli#native-deploy)

---

# Appflow CLI - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

note

Appflow CLI is available to our enterprise customers only

Appflow products can be used inside your own CI/CD platform (such as Azure DevOps, Gitlab, Jenkins, and more) through the use of our Appflow CLI.

What/who is the Appflow CLI for?[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#whatwho-is-the-appflow-cli-for "Direct link to What/who is the Appflow CLI for?")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Appflow CLI allows enterprises with their own CI/CD platforms to have granular control of Appflow within their existing pipelines. Rather than setting up automations using Appflow Automations, enterprises can utilize the CLI to control exactly when builds are produced and deployed, so they insert their own processes in between these steps (e.g. code signing, QA testing, manager approvals, etc.)

### Install[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#install "Direct link to Install")

To install, run the following command

    curl -fsSL https://ionic.io/get-appflow-cli | bash

#### Locking the Appflow CLI Version[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#locking-the-appflow-cli-version "Direct link to Locking the Appflow CLI Version")

If you'd like to lock the version of Appflow CLI in place for your pipeline, use the following command to install the Appflow CLI instead, replacing `X.X.X` with your desired version number:

    export APPFLOW_VERSION=X.X.X; curl -fsSL https://ionic.io/get-appflow-cli | bash

### Authentication[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#authentication "Direct link to Authentication")

Before you use the Appflow CLI you'll first need a [Personal Access Token](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/personal-access-tokens)
.

Once you have your token, you can authenticate the Appflow CLI by setting the token to an environment variable in you CI/CD system.

    export IONIC_TOKEN=mytoken

or you can create an Appflow CLI config file:

    # .appflow.yamlTOKEN: my-token

and run your commands from the directory the file is in, or by specifying the location with the `--config=/some/path/to/.appflow.yaml` flag.

[![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-quickstart-icon.png)![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-quickstart-icon.png)\
\
Before using the Appflow CLI, you must set up a Personal Access Token to authenticate with Ionic.](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/personal-access-tokens)
[![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-deploy-icon.png)![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-deploy-icon.png)\
\
Update your app remotely in real-time using your own CI/CD pipelines.](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/live-updates)
[![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-package-icon.png)![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-package-icon.png)\
\
Build app binaries in the cloud for iOS and Android, even on your linux CI/CD runner.](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds)
[![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-automate-icon.png)![](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/icons/guide-automate-icon.png)\
\
Publish directly to the iOS App Store and Google Play Store from your own CI/CD platform in one command.](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/app-store-deploy)

Why a separate CLI?[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/overview#why-a-separate-cli "Direct link to Why a separate CLI?")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It was a difficult decision, but ultimately we decided to split Appflow related CLI commands into its own CLI for three reasons:

1.  **Simplification of CLIs**. Ionic Framework and Capacitor commands make sense to have under one roof because they both serve the same use case (assisting a developer while they are developing), however the Appflow related commands serve a completely different use case (allowing an external CI/CD system to control when builds or deployments are made).
    
2.  **Reduce installation size**. Ionic CLI requires node (and npm) to install. For development, a user would already have those installed, however for a CI/CD system interacting with Appflow, a dependency like Node may be completely unnecessary and causes pipelines to take longer and use more resources. Appflow CLI is installed in one command and is around 10 MB in size.
    
3.  **Better DevOps practices**. We used moving to a separate CLI to increase clarity in the names of the commands and their function for more granular control of your DevOps pipeline e.g. `deploy/package build` to `build <web,android,ios>` and `deploy <web,android,ios>`.

---

# 0.x to 1.x Migration Guide - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/0x-1x#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Version 1.0 of the Appflow CLI has been released, and there are some notable changes, first of which is the rename of the CLI from `ionic-cloud` to `appflow`.

Deprecations[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/0x-1x#deprecations "Direct link to Deprecations")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Going forward, the default command for the CLI will be `appflow` instead of `ionic-cloud`. However, 1.x versions of the Appflow CLI will alias appflow to `ionic-cloud`, so you can continue to use `ionic-cloud`.

The installation URL and environment variable for pinning the CLI version are also changing, but will have a fallback for 1.x releases.

    export APPFLOW_VERSION=X.X.X; curl -fsSL https://ionic.io/get-appflow-cli | bash

If the CLI was installed locally, the previous installation will need to be removed before executing the previous command.

    rm -f /usr/local/bin/ionic-cloud

Breaking Changes[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/0x-1x#breaking-changes "Direct link to Breaking Changes")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The `delete build` command is being replaced in favor of [`build cancel`](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_build_cancel)
    .
*   The `get build` command is being replaced in favor of [`build get`](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_build_get)
    .
    *   **Note:** the `artifacts` object in the command output has been updated as well.
*   The `destination create` command is being replaced in favor of [`live-update create-channel`](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_live-update_create-channel)
    .

Contents
--------

*   [Deprecations](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/0x-1x#deprecations)
    
*   [Breaking Changes](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/migrations/0x-1x#breaking-changes)

---

# Parsing --json Output from Appflow CLI - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Introduction[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#introduction "Direct link to Introduction")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When including `--json` as a flag for a `build` or `deploy`, several helpful pieces of information will be returned once the command finishes running in a parsable JSON format.

This is extremely useful when performing separate `build` and `deploy` commands where you'd like to deploy the newly built artifact.

Saving a Variable[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#saving-a-variable "Direct link to Saving a Variable")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First, let's perform a Build utilizing the Appflow CLI like so (be sure to replace your own `--app-id`, `--commit`, and `--token`):

    appflow build android debug --app-id AppIdEx123abc --commit abc123 --token $(IONIC_TOKEN) --json

Running this command (or any other command) with `--json` will show you the specific JSON that is returned from that command.

In this case we're performing a build, so let's grab the `buildId` and save it to a variable using `jq` (preinstalled on most CI/CD platforms).

    BUILDID=$(appflow build android debug --app-id AppIdEx123abc --commit abc123 --token $(IONIC_TOKEN) --json  | jq -r '.buildId')

Using a Variable Later[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#using-a-variable-later "Direct link to Using a Variable Later")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `BUILDID` bash variable should now be available to use in the next command. An example of that would be the following (where you've already [set up a Destination](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/destinations/google)
 called "GooglePlay"):

    appflow deploy android --app-id AppIdEx123abc --build-id $(BUILDID) --destination "GooglePlay" --token $(IONIC_TOKEN)

Contents
--------

*   [Introduction](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#introduction)
    
*   [Saving a Variable](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#saving-a-variable)
    
*   [Using a Variable Later](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/examples/parsing-output#using-a-variable-later)

# Native Builds - Appflow

[Skip to main content](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

note

Appflow CLI is available to our enterprise customers only

### Authentication[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#authentication "Direct link to Authentication")

The Appflow CLI has to be authenticated on Appflow to be able to trigger a native build.

To log in, create a [Personal Access Token](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/personal-access-tokens)
 on the Appflow Dashboard.

then type:

    $ export IONIC_TOKEN=<your personal access token>

### Trigger a native build[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#trigger-a-native-build "Direct link to Trigger a native build")

Assuming the CLI is authenticated, to trigger a native build, run:

    $ appflow build <ios|android> <build_type> --app-id=<appflow app id> --commit=<git commit sha> --signing-cert="My Signing Certificate"

**Build type** can be `simulator`, `ad-hoc`, `app-store`, `development` or `enterprise` for iOS and `debug` or `release` for Android.

Signing cert is required for all build types except `debug` on Android. For information on setting up signing certificates, see [here](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/package/credentials)
.

Once the build is successfully triggered, the CLI will automatically start tailing the logs from Appflow.

### Customize the native build with Options[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#customize-the-native-build-with-options "Direct link to Customize the native build with Options")

Noteable options available to customize any native build include:

*   `--build-stack=<name>` to override the target [build stack](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/build-stacks)
    , defaults to latest stack
*   `--environment=<name>` to specify the group of [environment variables](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/package/environments)
     to be exposed to the build
*   `--env <list of key value pairs>` Specify [ad-hoc environment variables](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/package/environments#ad-hoc-environments)
    . You can use the flag any number of times to specify as many environment variables.
*   `--env-file=<name>` to specify a file with any number of environment variables (one on each line).
*   `--secret <list of key value pairs>` to pass ad-hoc secrets into the build process
*   `--native-config=<name>` to specify the group of [native config variables](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/package/native-configs)
     to be exposed to your build
*   `--signing-cert` to specify the name of a [signing certificate](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/package/adding-credentials)
     (for build types that require it)
*   `--dependency-caching` to enable [caching](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/tutorial/caching)
     of npm packages to accelerate the build process
*   `--native-app-preview` to enable an [app preview](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/previews/native-previews#native-app-previews)
     directly in the browser via a secure public URL using Appetize (for debug build types only)

### Customize the native build with Advanced Options[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#customize-the-native-build-with-advanced-options "Direct link to Customize the native build with Advanced Options")

Noteable options available to customize an iOS build include:

*   `--ipa` Download iOS IPA artifact
*   `--xcarchive` Download xcarchive artifact
*   `--dsym` Download iOS DSYM artifact

Additional options can be be explored in the [CLI Command Reference](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_build_ios)

Noteable options available to customize an android build include:

*   `--apk` Download Android APK artifact
*   `--aab` Download Android AAB artifact

Additional options can be be explored in the [CLI Command Reference](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/reference/appflow_build_android)

### Note about referencing Options values by name[​](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#note-about-referencing-options-values-by-name "Direct link to Note about referencing Options values by name")

Names are case-sensitive and need to be specified including spaces, for instance:

    $ appflow build ios development --app-id=123 --commit=456 --signing-cert="iOS Dev"

Look for the name on the Signing Certificate, Environments, and Native Configs pages in Appflow:

![Signing Certificates](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/assets/images/cli-security-profile-list-ee09cd373a8ddebd56125fb0908266b0.png) ![Environments](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/assets/images/cli-environments-list-6ac29eaa96588f7296ea5cd5a088460f.png) ![Native Configs](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/assets/images/cli-native-config-list-cfe7ca6f7818daa529c3c90a958e83c3.png)

For the build stack, use one of the strings identifying the platform in the Appflow Build page:

![Target Platform](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/assets/images/cli-target-platform-f051dba32e494f560c6f1a539fa1a980.png)

Contents
--------

*   [Authentication](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#authentication)
    
*   [Trigger a native build](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#trigger-a-native-build)
    
*   [Customize the native build with Options](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#customize-the-native-build-with-options)
    
*   [Customize the native build with Advanced Options](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#customize-the-native-build-with-advanced-options)
    
*   [Note about referencing Options values by name](https://ionic.io/docs/appflow/cli/native-builds#note-about-referencing-options-values-by-name)
